config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, abuse becomes easy to justify. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting people’s dignity, freedom, and legal rights first, then balancing those rights with the common good. In practice, I believe the state and the majority should have clear limits, even when they claim to act for society’s benefit.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu kalau dimaknai sebagai hak individu harus tunduk pada kepentingan masyarakat secara umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat bahaya besar ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi kuasa terlalu luas atas nama “kebaikan bersama.” Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru hak-hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari diskriminasi harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dengan melindungi martabat dan kebebasan orang, bukan dengan menganggap individu sekadar alat untuk tujuan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement, because “society first” is too easy to use as a justification for ignoring minority rights and silencing people. In Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to public order, harmony, or moral values can be used to pressure individuals, so I do not trust the idea that the collective should automatically outrank the person. Society does matter, but it should be served by limiting power and protecting basic rights, not by treating individuals as disposable for the majority. If anything, a strong society is one that can balance the common good without trampling personal dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dipahami bahwa kepentingan masyarakat boleh mengalahkan hak individu secara umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk membungkus pembatasan hak dengan dalih “kebaikan bersama.” Hak individu memang tidak tanpa batas, tetapi kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari diskriminasi harus menjadi garis merah yang tidak gampang digeser. Poin tentang menjaga ketertiban dan harmoni memang penting, tetapi bagi saya masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dengan membatasi kuasa kolektif, bukan dengan menempatkan orang sebagai alat demi tujuan sosial. Kalau hak minoritas bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa perlu, itu bukan keseimbangan, melainkan risiko penyalahgunaan yang serius.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because in Indonesia that kind of thinking often becomes a shortcut for silencing dissent and pressuring minorities. Public order, harmony, and shared values do matter, but I have seen how easily they can be used to excuse unfair treatment when no clear limits protect the person. A society is not truly strong if it asks individuals to sacrifice basic dignity every time the majority wants convenience or control. For me, the better principle is that the common good should be pursued within firm boundaries of individual rights, not above them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari perspektif saya di Amerika, memberi prioritas pada masyarakat di atas hak individu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Poin tentang ketertiban umum dan kebaikan bersama memang penting, tetapi saya tidak percaya itu boleh dipakai untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, atau perlindungan minoritas. Kalau hak orang bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu demi kepentingan sosial, maka yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan masyarakat yang memberi terlalu banyak kuasa pada negara dan massa. Bagi saya, kepentingan bersama harus dicapai dengan menghormati batas-batas hak dasar, bukan dengan menempatkan individu sebagai alat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
